name: Alberta
funFact: Alberta holds most of Canada's oil sands reserves and is home to the Canadian Rockies.

name: British Columbia
funFact: Victoria, the capital, sits on Vancouver Island — not on the BC mainland.

name: Manitoba
funFact: Winnipeg sits at 'The Forks,' the historic meeting point of the Red and Assiniboine rivers.

name: New Brunswick
funFact: New Brunswick is Canada's only officially bilingual province, in both English and French.

name: Newfoundland and Labrador
funFact: St. John's is one of the oldest European-founded cities in North America.

name: Northwest Territories
funFact: Yellowknife sees near round-the-clock daylight in summer and vivid aurora displays in winter.

name: Nova Scotia
funFact: Halifax has one of the world's largest natural harbours.

name: Nunavut
funFact: Created in 1999, Nunavut is Canada's largest and northernmost territory, with Inuktitut as an official language.

name: Ontario
funFact: Ontario is home to both Ottawa, Canada's national capital, and Toronto, its most populous city.

name: Prince Edward Island
funFact: Canada's smallest province, PEI is the setting of the novel Anne of Green Gables.

name: Quebec
funFact: Quebec City's old town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the only walled city north of Mexico.

name: Saskatchewan
funFact: Saskatchewan is the only Canadian province with entirely man-made borders — no natural boundaries.

name: Yukon
funFact: Yukon is home to Mount Logan, Canada's highest peak.
